2604.11403.title: One Scale at a Time: Scale-Autoregressive Modeling for Fluid Flow Distributions
2604.11403.paper_summary: Analyzing unsteady fluid flows often requires access to the full distribution of possible temporal states, yet conventional PDE solvers are computationally prohibitive and learned time-stepping surrogates quickly accumulate error over long rollouts. Generative models avoid compounding error by sampling states independently, but diffusion and flow-matching methods, while accurate, are limited by the cost of many evaluations over the entire mesh. We introduce scale-autoregressive modeling (SAR) for sampling flows on unstructured meshes hierarchically from coarse to fine: it first generates a low-resolution field, then refines it by progressively sampling higher resolutions conditioned on coarser predictions. This coarse-to-fine factorization improves efficiency by concentrating computation at coarser scales, where uncertainty is greatest, while requiring fewer steps at finer scales. Across unsteady-flow benchmarks of varying complexity, SAR attains substantially lower distributional error and higher per-sample accuracy than state-of-the-art diffusion models based on multi-scale GNNs, while matching or surpassing a flow-matching Transolver (a linear-time transformer) yet running 2-7x faster than this depending on the task. Overall, SAR provides a practical tool for fast and accurate estimation of statistical flow quantities (e.g., turbulent kinetic energy and two-point correlations) in real-world settings.
2604.11403.paper_summary_zh: 分析不穩定流體流動通常需要獲取可能的時間狀態的完整分佈，然而傳統的偏微分方程求解器在計算上是不可行的，而學習的時間步進替代品在長時間的推演中迅速累積誤差。生成模型通過獨立取樣狀態來避免誤差的累積，但擴散和流匹配方法雖然準確，卻受到在整個網格上進行多次評估的成本限制。我們引入了尺度自回歸建模（SAR），以分層方式在非結構化網格上從粗到細取樣流動：它首先生成一個低解析度的場，然後通過逐步取樣基於較粗預測的更高解析度來進行細化。這種從粗到細的分解通過將計算集中在不確定性最大的粗糙尺度上來提高效率，同時在較細的尺度上需要更少的步驟。在各種複雜度的不穩定流動基準測試中，SAR達到了顯著較低的分佈誤差和較高的每樣本準確性，優於基於多尺度GNN的最先進擴散模型，同時匹配或超越流匹配Transolver（線性時間Transformer），而在這取決於任務的情況下運行速度比其快2-7倍。總體而言，SAR為在現實世界環境中快速準確地估計統計流量量（例如，湍流動能和二點相關性）提供了一個實用的工具。
2604.11403.author: Mario Lino et.al.
